category_title: Students who engage in active instructional avoidance through disruptive and off-task behaviors
minimal_context: Martin is participating in an independent reading block where the expectation is to read assigned materials quietly.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Martin, it is time for independent reading. Please open your assigned reading materials and work quietly.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Turns his body completely away from the assigned reading materials and looks around the room)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Martin, please focus on your reading.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stands up from his desk and walks over to the student sitting next to him) Hey, what are you looking at?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Martin, the expectation right now is to be quiet.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Remains out of his seat, keeping his eyes and body directed away from his own desk, and continues talking to his peer) I don't want to read, let's talk about recess.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Martin, it is time for independent reading. Please open your assigned reading materials and work quietly.

STUDENT: (Turns his body completely away from the assigned reading materials and looks around the room)

TUTOR: Martin, please focus on your reading.

STUDENT: (Stands up from his desk and walks over to the student sitting next to him) Hey, what are you looking at?

TUTOR: Martin, the expectation right now is to be quiet.

STUDENT: (Remains out of his seat, keeping his eyes and body directed away from his own desk, and continues talking to his peer) I don't want to read, let's talk about recess.

task.learning_goal: Working quietly, having needed materials, and completing assigned work

context.environment: Independent reading where the expectation was to be quiet
context.materials: Assigned reading materials, break card kept on the desk, daily progress report, materials for doodling or coloring

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: During instructional activities when expected to engage with assigned reading materials
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Off-task behavior, defined as out of seat and or eyes/body directed away from assigned materials, and talking to peers

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Elevated rates of problem behavior—off-task behavior was scored in approximately 54% of intervals, disruption in 30% of intervals, and out of seat in 16% of intervals
